title: Gemini API Gets Flex, Priority Tiers
summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers to balance cost and latency in the Gemini API. This move aims to provide developers with more options for optimizing their applications.
body:
Google has announced the introduction of two new inference tiers, Flex and Priority, to the Gemini API. The Flex tier is designed to provide a cost-effective option for applications with flexible latency requirements, while the Priority tier offers lower latency for applications that require faster response times. 

 The introduction of these new tiers is significant as it allows developers to better balance cost and reliability in their applications. By choosing the appropriate tier, developers can optimize their applications for either cost or performance, depending on their specific needs. This move is expected to have a positive impact on the development of AI-powered applications, as it provides more flexibility and control over resource allocation. 

 The reaction to the introduction of the new tiers has been positive, with many developers welcoming the additional options for optimizing their applications. As the AI landscape continues to evolve, it will be interesting to see how these new tiers are utilized and what impact they have on the development of AI-powered applications.
